Doctor 2.description: conversing about an ER doctor, Dr. Patel, asking a patient about a severe headache that started suddenly around 6 a.m., its 0–10 intensity, and associated symptoms, with the patient reporting 6/10 pain plus dizziness, nausea, light/noise sensitivity, and mental fogginess @ <waiting> 23 2
chat: Hi, I’m Dr. Patel—one of the ER doctors. I understand you’ve had a severe headache since around 6 a.m.; can you tell me exactly when it started, how bad it is from 0–10, and whether you’ve had any dizziness, nausea, light/noise sensitivity, or vision changes?

Yeah, it hit me right around 6 this morning, kind of all at once. I’d say it’s about a 6 out of 10, and I’ve been really dizzy and nauseous, and the lights and sounds feel way too intense. My head feels foggy too—like I can’t think straight.
